brief: A personal page telling an applied-ML career as one continuous line riding the field’s real epochs — deep learning, transformers, LLMs, agents — stations with shipped outcomes, promotions where the line steps up, and the one deliberate reversal out of management back to the tools.

arguments.contentBrief: A personal page telling an applied-ML career as one continuous line riding the field’s real epochs — deep learning, transformers, LLMs, agents — stations with shipped outcomes, promotions where the line steps up, and the one deliberate reversal out of management back to the tools.

structuredContent.rationale: Selected 'the-line' (home-career-project-timeline, art-directed style); shares audience(s) [personal-internal]; matched intent keyword(s) [showcase, career, trajectory, connect, projects, outcomes, personal]; art-directed style fits standard corporate suitability; motion preference 3 noted (the template locks its own motion).

sections.purpose: The sticky top chrome and footer identity of the personal page — the world label, the service line, and the REQUIRED synthetic-profile provenance notice.

spec.name: chrome.pageTitle
spec.guidance: The browser-tab title stem (assigned in JS, derived byte-identically; the template appends " — {person name} — Live"), e.g. "The Line".

slots.guidance: The browser-tab title stem (assigned in JS, derived byte-identically; the template appends " — {person name} — Live"), e.g. "The Line".

spec.name: chrome.world
spec.guidance: The world label on the top chrome, e.g. "THE LINE · PERSONAL PAGE".

slots.guidance: The world label on the top chrome, e.g. "THE LINE · PERSONAL PAGE".

spec.name: chrome.service
spec.guidance: The service line on the top chrome, e.g. "SERVICE · LINE OPEN 2014 · STILL RUNNING".

slots.guidance: The service line on the top chrome, e.g. "SERVICE · LINE OPEN 2014 · STILL RUNNING".

spec.name: chrome.syntheticMark
spec.guidance: REQUIRED provenance notice — the synthetic-mark badge in the chrome (and echoed in the footer). Must state the profile is illustrative/synthetic where entries are not real, e.g. "ILLUSTRATIVE PROFILE · SYNTHETIC".

slots.guidance: REQUIRED provenance notice — the synthetic-mark badge in the chrome (and echoed in the footer). Must state the profile is illustrative/synthetic where entries are not real, e.g. "ILLUSTRATIVE PROFILE · SYNTHETIC".

spec.name: chrome.footerProvenance
spec.guidance: The footer provenance line reinforcing the synthetic mark, e.g. "ILLUSTRATIVE PROFILE · SYNTHETIC · SAMPLE CONTENT IS MARKED AS SUCH".

slots.guidance: The footer provenance line reinforcing the synthetic mark, e.g. "ILLUSTRATIVE PROFILE · SYNTHETIC · SAMPLE CONTENT IS MARKED AS SUCH".
slots.example: ILLUSTRATIVE PROFILE · SYNTHETIC · SAMPLE CONTENT IS MARKED AS SUCH

spec.name: chrome.footerService
spec.guidance: The footer service line, e.g. "LINE OPEN 2014 · STILL RUNNING".

slots.guidance: The footer service line, e.g. "LINE OPEN 2014 · STILL RUNNING".

sections.purpose: The editorial hero — the kicker, the multi-line display statement, the person identity line, the subline, the accessible synthetic-profile notice, and the identity facts.

spec.name: hero.kicker
spec.guidance: The hero eyebrow, e.g. "THE LINE".

slots.guidance: The hero eyebrow, e.g. "THE LINE".

spec.name: hero.statementLines
spec.guidance: Two-to-four display lines (one per array entry, rendered as broken lines) building the career thesis, e.g. "One line," / "twelve years," / "no gaps hidden.".

slots.guidance: Two-to-four display lines (one per array entry, rendered as broken lines) building the career thesis, e.g. "One line," / "twelve years," / "no gaps hidden.".

spec.name: hero.person.name
spec.guidance: The person at the head of the line; a fact from the provided source (illustrative names are covered by the provenance notice), e.g. "Marcus Adeyemi".

slots.guidance: The person at the head of the line; a fact from the provided source (illustrative names are covered by the provenance notice), e.g. "Marcus Adeyemi".

spec.name: hero.person.role
spec.guidance: The person's current role, e.g. "Principal ML Engineer".

slots.guidance: The person's current role, e.g. "Principal ML Engineer".

spec.name: hero.person.team
spec.guidance: The person's current team, e.g. "Decisioning Platform".

slots.guidance: The person's current team, e.g. "Decisioning Platform".

spec.name: hero.person.location
spec.guidance: The person's location, e.g. "Melbourne".

slots.guidance: The person's location, e.g. "Melbourne".

spec.name: hero.subline
spec.guidance: One reading of the career under the statement, naming the honest detour, e.g. "A career is not a list of job titles — it is a single line that thickens where I grew, branches where I gambled, and once doubled back on itself. This is that line, drawn honestly, with the detour left in.".

slots.guidance: One reading of the career under the statement, naming the honest detour, e.g. "A career is not a list of job titles — it is a single line that thickens where I grew, branches where I gambled, and once doubled back on itself. This is that line, drawn honestly, with the detour left in.".
slots.example: A career is not a list of job titles — it is a single line that thickens where I grew, branches where I gambled, and once doubled back on itself. This is that line, drawn honestly, with the detour left in.

spec.name: hero.syntheticNotice
spec.guidance: The accessible (visually hidden) provenance paragraph stating the profile is a demonstration, e.g. "This entire profile is illustrative and synthetic — a demonstration person, not a real member of staff. Projects, dates and figures are sample content.".

slots.guidance: The accessible (visually hidden) provenance paragraph stating the profile is a demonstration, e.g. "This entire profile is illustrative and synthetic — a demonstration person, not a real member of staff. Projects, dates and figures are sample content.".
slots.example: This entire profile is illustrative and synthetic — a demonstration person, not a real member of staff. Projects, dates and figures are sample content.

spec.name: hero.facts
spec.guidance: Three-to-six identity facts (label, value) in the hero definition list, e.g. { label: "GAUGE", value: "analyst → principal" }.

slots.guidance: Three-to-six identity facts (label, value) in the hero definition list, e.g. { label: "GAUGE", value: "analyst → principal" }.

sections.purpose: The commanding survey line — the stations top-to-bottom with the one honest switchback flagged, the four-rung gauge-role ladder, the origin marker, and the next-station-under-survey terminus. The gauge ladder is a template-fixed map over four rungs; the fill re-labels the rungs and writes the stations.

spec.name: line.sectionTitle
spec.guidance: The §1 section heading, e.g. "THE LINE".

slots.guidance: The §1 section heading, e.g. "THE LINE".

spec.name: line.sectionSub
spec.guidance: The §1 section sub-line (the legend), true of the line you supply, e.g. "STATION = PROJECT · GAUGE = GRADE · BRANCH = SIDE-PROJECT · ONE SWITCHBACK, LEFT IN".

slots.guidance: The §1 section sub-line (the legend), true of the line you supply, e.g. "STATION = PROJECT · GAUGE = GRADE · BRANCH = SIDE-PROJECT · ONE SWITCHBACK, LEFT IN".
slots.example: STATION = PROJECT · GAUGE = GRADE · BRANCH = SIDE-PROJECT · ONE SWITCHBACK, LEFT IN

spec.name: line.originLabel
spec.guidance: The origin marker label where the line opens, e.g. "LINE OPEN · 2014".

slots.guidance: The origin marker label where the line opens, e.g. "LINE OPEN · 2014".

spec.name: line.gaugeRoles
spec.guidance: The four grade-rung labels the gauge maps to (an object keyed 1..4, the line thickening as it climbs). FIXED-SLOT: exactly the four rungs, e.g. { "1": "Analyst", "2": "Senior Analyst", "3": "Lead", "4": "Principal" }.

slots.guidance: The four grade-rung labels the gauge maps to (an object keyed 1..4, the line thickening as it climbs). FIXED-SLOT: exactly the four rungs, e.g. { "1": "Analyst", "2": "Senior Analyst", "3": "Lead", "4": "Principal" }.

spec.name: line.nodes
spec.guidance: Five-to-fourteen stations, earliest first (id, kind station|gauge-change|switchback|branch, code, year, name, gauge 1|2|3|4, outcome carrying a real number; promotion on a gauge-change; branch {fate terminated|rejoined, carried} on a branch; detour {span, lesson} on the switchback). Exactly ONE node is "switchback" — the single honest detour the page is arranged around, e.g. a two-year migration reversed out.

slots.guidance: Five-to-fourteen stations, earliest first (id, kind station|gauge-change|switchback|branch, code, year, name, gauge 1|2|3|4, outcome carrying a real number; promotion on a gauge-change; branch {fate terminated|rejoined, carried} on a branch; detour {span, lesson} on the switchback). Exactly ONE node is "switchback" — the single honest detour the page is arranged around, e.g. a two-year migration reversed out.

spec.name: line.terminus.code
spec.guidance: The terminus station code, e.g. "DP-10".

slots.guidance: The terminus station code, e.g. "DP-10".

spec.name: line.terminus.label
spec.guidance: The terminus label, e.g. "NEXT STATION · UNDER SURVEY".

slots.guidance: The terminus label, e.g. "NEXT STATION · UNDER SURVEY".

spec.name: line.terminus.name
spec.guidance: The next station under survey, e.g. "Causal decisioning".

slots.guidance: The next station under survey, e.g. "Causal decisioning".

spec.name: line.terminus.intent
spec.guidance: The terminus intent — stated as intent, not a promise, e.g. "Moving the bank from prediction to intervention — decisions that change outcomes, not just forecast them. Surveyed, not yet cut. Stated as intent, not as a promise.".

slots.guidance: The terminus intent — stated as intent, not a promise, e.g. "Moving the bank from prediction to intervention — decisions that change outcomes, not just forecast them. Surveyed, not yet cut. Stated as intent, not as a promise.".
slots.example: Moving the bank from prediction to intervention — decisions that change outcomes, not just forecast them. Surveyed, not yet cut. Stated as intent, not as a promise.

sections.purpose: The §2 interchanges panel — where this line crossed other people's lines.

spec.name: interchange.sectionTitle
spec.guidance: The §2 section heading, e.g. "INTERCHANGES".

slots.guidance: The §2 section heading, e.g. "INTERCHANGES".

spec.name: interchange.sectionSub
spec.guidance: The §2 section sub-line, e.g. "WHERE THIS LINE CROSSED OTHER PEOPLE'S".

slots.guidance: The §2 section sub-line, e.g. "WHERE THIS LINE CROSSED OTHER PEOPLE'S".
slots.example: WHERE THIS LINE CROSSED OTHER PEOPLE

spec.name: interchange.items
spec.guidance: Two-to-six interchanges (id, code, station, crossedWith, note), e.g. { code: "IX-B", station: "DP-06 · ML guild", crossedWith: "Priya Menon — Streaming Platform", note: "Her pipelines under my models; we shared an on-call rotation for a year." }.

slots.guidance: Two-to-six interchanges (id, code, station, crossedWith, note), e.g. { code: "IX-B", station: "DP-06 · ML guild", crossedWith: "Priya Menon — Streaming Platform", note: "Her pipelines under my models; we shared an on-call rotation for a year." }.

sections.purpose: The §3 station register — the line as a dated table (rendered from line.nodes + terminus); this section carries only its heading and the accessible caption.

spec.name: register.sectionTitle
spec.guidance: The §3 section heading, e.g. "STATION REGISTER".

slots.guidance: The §3 section heading, e.g. "STATION REGISTER".

spec.name: register.sectionSub
spec.guidance: The §3 section sub-line, e.g. "THE LINE AS A DATED TABLE — THE ACCESSIBLE MIRROR".

slots.guidance: The §3 section sub-line, e.g. "THE LINE AS A DATED TABLE — THE ACCESSIBLE MIRROR".
slots.example: THE LINE AS A DATED TABLE — THE ACCESSIBLE MIRROR

spec.name: register.caption
spec.guidance: The accessible caption of the register table naming its columns, e.g. "Every station on the line in date order: code, year, project, grade in force, and outcome. The dated equivalent of the drawn line above.".

slots.guidance: The accessible caption of the register table naming its columns, e.g. "Every station on the line in date order: code, year, project, grade in force, and outcome. The dated equivalent of the drawn line above.".
slots.example: Every station on the line in date order: code, year, project, grade in force, and outcome. The dated equivalent of the drawn line above.

craftGuarantees: A career staged as ONE continuous survey line on a dark slate cartographic field — cold and vertical, a single luminous aqua rail that thickens with grade (gauge), branches for side-projects, and once doubles back on itself. Station typography is mono; project names are an editorial serif.

The commanding visual is THE LINE: every project placed as a STATION down the years, promotions drawn as GAUGE CHANGES where the rail visibly thickens, side-projects as BRANCH lines that terminate honestly or rejoin carrying something back. THE GAUGE LADDER IS FIXED-SLOT — line weight and dot size are a template-fixed map over four rungs, so the schema pins the gauge-role label map to exactly those four rungs; a fill re-labels the rungs (any four-rung ladder) but cannot add a fifth weight the geometry has nowhere to draw. Any schema-valid fill renders composed.

Exactly one line node carries kind "switchback": the single honest detour the whole page is arranged around — a reversed-out chapter drawn as a loop on the rail and labelled with what it taught, and flagged in the mirror table. The flag is STRUCTURAL (a kind field the schema requires exactly one of), never a match on free-form text.

PERSONAL-PAGE HONESTY: this surface presents a PERSON. The schema never forces invented biography — the facts (name, roles, projects, dates, figures) come from the source the fill author was given. Where entries are illustrative or synthetic, the REQUIRED provenance notice (chrome.syntheticMark) says so plainly; it prints as the chrome badge and is reinforced in the footer and the accessible hero notice. The one switchback is an honesty device — a career drawn with a real detour left in, not airbrushed.

The drawn line is decorative (aria-hidden rail); the REAL content is the visible §3 station-register TABLE — the line as a dated register — so the world is fully legible without the rail. The terminus states the next station as INTENT, not a promise.

Slot char caps and item counts are sized so any schema-valid fill stays composed — the display statement never overflows, the rail and its branches stay balanced, and the register table stays legible. Motion: the line draws in on a token easing (DataInkDraw, signature data-ink-draw); fully drawn and static under reduced motion. The mood is locked dark.

The drawn line is decorative (aria-hidden rail); the REAL content is the visible §3 station-register TABLE — the line as a dated register — so the world is fully legible without the rail. The terminus states the next station as INTENT, not a promise.

Slot char caps and item counts are sized so any schema-valid fill stays composed — the display statement never overflows, the rail and its branches stay balanced, and the register table stays legible. Motion: the line draws in on a token easing (DataInkDraw, signature data-ink-draw); fully drawn and static under reduced motion. The mood is locked dark.
